texto: Children discover and verify their theories in quite the same way that scientists do: through experimentation. They manipulate the world and discover regularities of causation from those manipulations. Why do they do it? The discovery of regularities comes with a pleasurable burst of insight, which all of us, but especially children and scientists, continuously long for like bonbons or opium.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Matthew M. Hurley: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “Children discover and verify their theories in quite the same way that scientists do: through exp…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, cognitive-science, discovery pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de discovery e science.
